events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The user wants me to act as a tradesperson/inspector assessing a commercial electrical job. I must produce a single valid JSON object to /app/answer.json. The scenario: Lighting controls & emergency egress, observed field condition: 3-way miswire diagnosis, work stage tested/inspected. Reference: NEC 700, NFPA 101.

I need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. A 3-way miswire in lighting controls/emergency egress is non-compliant and potentially hazardous (fail). Risk: high or critical. Since emergency egress lighting and controls may be affected by miswire, it could impair emergency illumination, so critical is appropriate, but high is also defensible. The schema says risk low/medium/high/critical. Given life-safety emergency egress, I'd choose critical.

Work_stage: tested/inspected (given).
Component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent_complete: probably around 90 but defective tested; maybe 95. I'll put 90.
Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked).
Sound_source, event_time, rate, order: defaults.
Workable: empty list (not asked what can still start).
Findings: 3-way switch miswire causing improper control/operation of emergency egress lighting; may result in loss of emergency illumination; violates NEC 700.12/700.15 and NFPA 101 egress illumination requirements.
Actions: Correct wiring per approved plans/manufacturer instructions; retest 3-way and emergency lighting transfer; verify normal/emergency operation at each switch location; re-inspect.
Rationale: Miswired 3-way control compromises required emergency egress lighting operation, is non-compliant with NEC 700 and NFPA 101, and must be corrected.
References: NEC 700.12 (emergency system power and lighting), NEC 700.15 (emergency circuits), NFPA 101 (means of egress illumination/exit signs).

Need to ensure JSON valid, no code fences, no prose.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "3-way switching miswire identified during testing, causing improper control of emergency egress lighting circuits.",
    "Miswire may prevent required automatic transfer or maintained illumination of emergency egress lights during normal power loss.",
    "Condition is non-compliant with emergency system wiring and reliability requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Correct the 3-way switch wiring per the approved shop drawings and manufacturer instructions.",
    "Retest all affected 3-way control points for proper normal and emergency operation.",
    "Verify emergency egress lights illuminate automatically upon loss of normal power and remain energized as required.",
    "Schedule re-inspection with the third-party inspector before energizing for occupancy."
  ],
  "rationale": "A 3-way miswire in emergency egress lighting controls is a life-safety defect: it can prevent required emergency illumination during a power failure, violating NEC 700 emergency system requirements and NFPA 101 means of egress illumination requirements. The work must fail inspection until corrected and retested.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 700.12 - Emergency system power sources and illumination requirements",
    "NEC 700.15 - Emergency circuits and wiring methods",
    "NFPA 101 - Means of egress illumination and emergency lighting requirements"
  ]
}
